_comment: DO NOT EDIT: This file was auto-generated by Pterodactyl Egg Exporter
name: Yuzuki MD v2
author: your@email.com
description: Yuzuki MD v2 — WhatsApp bot built with Baileys. Uses pairing code authentication (no QR scan needed). Set PHONE_NUMBER in variables, start the server, then link via WhatsApp → Settings → Linked Devices → Link with phone number.

installation.script:
#!/bin/bash
# Yuzuki MD v2 - Pterodactyl Install Script
apt-get update -y
apt-get install -y git curl ffmpeg python3 build-essential libfontconfig1 libfreetype6

# Install Node.js 22 if not already present
if ! command -v node &> /dev/null; then
  curl -fsSL https://deb.nodesource.com/setup_22.x | bash -
  apt-get install -y nodejs
fi

node -v
npm -v

# Install dependencies
cd /home/container
npm install

echo "Installation complete! Start the server to get your pairing code."

variables.name: Phone Number
variables.description: Your WhatsApp phone number in full international format, digits only. Example: 628123456789 (no +, spaces, or dashes). The bot will display a pairing code in the console — enter it in WhatsApp → Settings → Linked Devices → Link with phone number.

variables.name: Log Level
variables.description: Pino log level. Options: trace, debug, info, warn, error. Default: info

variables.name: OpenAI API Key
variables.description: Optional. Enables .gpt and other OpenAI-powered commands. Get from platform.openai.com

variables.name: Anthropic API Key
variables.description: Optional. Enables .claude command. Get from console.anthropic.com

variables.name: Gemini API Key
variables.description: Optional. Enables .gemini command. Get from aistudio.google.com

variables.name: Node Version
variables.description: The node version to use. Do not change this unless you know what you are doing.
